## How Do I Get Started? ##

1. [Create a fork](https://docs.github.com/en/get-started/quickstart/fork-a-repo)
2. [Clone your fork](https://docs.github.com/en/get-started/quickstart/fork-a-repo)
3. [Create a branch](https://docs.github.com/en/get-started/quickstart/fork-a-repo)
4. [Make changes](https://docs.github.com/en/get-started/quickstart/fork-a-repo)
5. [Submit a pull request](https://docs.github.com/en/get-started/quickstart/fork-a-repo)

## Rules ##

Please, accept the following rules:

### Content General Requirements ###

- [ ] I will check publication dates, updates, and software versions.
- [ ] I will check for typos and grammar errors.
- [ ] I will check for broken links and broken images.
- [ ] I will provide each section with a heading that reflects the essence of the content.
- [ ] I will structure the content: ensure clear division into sections, subsections, and blocks.
- [ ] I will maintain a consistent writing style: tone, terminology, abbreviations.
- [ ] I will write the text in clear language, avoiding excessive jargon.

### Text Formatting ###

- [ ] I will use Markdown formatting.
- [ ] I will avoid using HTML formatting.
- [ ] I will use bulleted or numbered lists to enumerate steps, features, or benefits.
- [ ] I will format code in syntax‑highlighted blocks.
- [ ] I will keep paragraphs short and add spacing between them.
- [ ] I will place quotes or important notes in separate blocks.

### Review and Publication ###

- [ ] I will proofread the text for errors: spelling, punctuation, style.
- [ ] I will test the page across different browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge).
- [ ] I will obtain approval from the content owner (editor, team lead) before publishing.
- [ ] After publication, I will verify correct display using live data.
- [ ] I will schedule regular updates.

## Public SSH key ##

To grant quick repository access, here's my public SSH key (`id_rsa.pub` or equivalent):

```text
<paste your public SSH key here>
```

1. If you haven’t created an SSH key yet, follow the [official GitHub guide](https://docs.github.com/en/authentication/connecting-to-github-with-ssh/generating-a-new-ssh-key-and-adding-it-to-the-ssh-agent).
2. Copy the contents of your public key (usually located at `~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub` or `~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub`).
3. Paste the key below (it should start with `ssh-rsa` or `ssh-ed25519`).

## After submission ##

Our project coordinator will review your request and:

- Add you to the organization/repository (if required).
- Confirm access.
- Reply to this issue with further instructions.

Thank you for your contribution! ✨
